edges.description: BCL2 translocation blocks germinal center apoptosis
MYC drives rapid proliferation of lymphoma cells
Accumulation of large B-cell lymphoma in lymph nodes manifests as lymphadenopathy.
Systemic lymphoma burden manifests as night sweats.
Systemic lymphoma burden manifests as weight loss.
Systemic lymphoma burden and inflammation manifest as fatigue.
Extranodal or lymphoid-organ lymphoma accumulation can manifest as splenomegaly.
Systemic lymphoma burden can manifest as anemia.

nodes.description: Anemia may result from bone marrow infiltration, hemolysis, or cytokine effects.

BCL2 translocation t(14;18) occurs in approximately 30% of GCB-DLBCL, placing BCL2 under immunoglobulin enhancer control. Resulting BCL2 overexpression blocks the mitochondrial apoptosis pathway, allowing survival of cells that would normally die in the germinal center.
Fatigue from disease burden, cytokine release, and anemia.

DLBCL arises from germinal center or post-germinal center B cells undergoing somatic hypermutation and class switch recombination. These processes introduce DNA double-strand breaks that can lead to oncogenic translocations. The cell of origin determines molecular subtype and clinical behavior.
Rapidly enlarging lymphadenopathy is the most common presentation. Nodal disease may be localized or widespread.

evidence.snippet: Patients most often present with a rapidly growing tumour mass in single or multiple, nodal or extranodal sites.

nodes.description: The combination of blocked apoptosis, enhanced proliferation, and constitutive survival signaling leads to aggressive expansion of large B-cell lymphoma involving lymph nodes and extranodal sites.

MYC rearrangements occur in 10-15% of DLBCL, most commonly with IGH but also with non-immunoglobulin partners. MYC overexpression drives cellular proliferation and metabolism. When combined with BCL2 rearrangement (double-hit lymphoma), creates highly aggressive disease.

ABC-DLBCL shows constitutive NF-kB activation through various mechanisms including BCR signaling mutations (CD79A/B), CARD11 mutations, MYD88 L265P mutation, or A20 deletion. NF-kB promotes survival and contributes to the aggressive behavior of ABC subtype.
B symptoms including drenching night sweats indicate active disease and are incorporated into staging.
Spleen involvement occurs in some cases, particularly with advanced stage disease.
Unintentional weight loss greater than 10% body weight in 6 months is a B symptom with prognostic significance.
